chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 124 –Arrival of Abu Muhammad Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/152)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/152)

parts.text: أَبِي عَلِيٍّ الْمُطَهَّرِ أَنَّهُ كَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ سَنَةَ الْقَادِسِيَّةِ يُعْلِمُهُ انْصِرَافَ النَّاسِ
أَنَّهُ يَخَافُ الْعَطَشَ فَكَتَبَ ( عليه السلام )

text: امْضُوا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَمَضَوْا سَالِمِينَ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Abu Abdullah Bin Salih, from his father, from Abu Ali Al Mutahhir,

‘He wrote to him<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) in the year of Al-Qadisiyya to let him<sup>asws</sup> know of the leaving (not performing Hajj) of the people and that he feared the thirst (due to the drought). So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘Continue, and there would be no fear upon you, if Allah<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires’. So Suleyman continued (to Hajj), and the Praise is for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the worlds’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali has narrated from abu Ahmad ibn Rashid from abu Hashim al-Ja‘fari who has said the following. “I requested abu Muhammad for something that I needed. He scratched the earth with his whip. The narrator has said that I think he then covered it with a handkerchief and then took out five hundred Dinars. He then said, “O abu Hashim take it and grants us pardon.”
